text.title: VR Environment Anchoring and Scale Rules
text.summary: Ground plane, horizon, scale, lighting, boundary, and grounding requirements that form the stable perceptual foundation for any VR experience.

rule.statement: Keep the floor/ground plane visible at all times with consistent appearance. Never float the user in a void without a stable reference surface.
rule.rationale: Loss of ground reference causes immediate disorientation; the ground plane is the primary spatial anchor for the vestibular system in VR.

rule.statement: Maintain a stable, expected horizon position. The horizon provides orientation and reduces nausea independently of the ground plane.
rule.rationale: An unstable horizon forces the vestibular and visual systems into conflict, the primary mechanism of VR sickness.

rule.statement: Include at least one stable reference object (cockpit in vehicle sims, dashboard in driving, wrist-mounted UI) that moves with the user and is always visible.
rule.rationale: Fixed reference objects provide a visual rest point that decouples perceived self-motion from environment motion, reducing visual-vestibular mismatch.

rule.statement: Use 1:1 real-world scale for familiar objects (doors 2 m, tables 70-75 cm, chairs 45 cm, average human height). When using intentionally different scale, make the difference unambiguous and provide a human-scale reference object.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous scale breaks depth perception and comfortable interaction distance; users reach for objects at the wrong distance when scale cues are inconsistent.

rule.statement: Use consistent lighting that matches real-world expectations for time of day; keep light sources visible; ensure shadows enhance depth. Avoid extremes: too dark breaks visibility, too bright causes discomfort.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent lighting undermines depth perception and increases the cognitive load needed to understand the environment.

rule.statement: Implement a guardian/chaperone system: visual boundary at wall approach, controller haptic warning, audio alert option, experience pause on boundary crossing, easy re-centering action.
rule.rationale: Physical collisions with real-world objects cause injury; guardian systems are required by all major platform certification programmes.

rule.statement: Define minimum play space requirements at first launch; warn if the space is too small and offer a seated alternative automatically.
rule.rationale: Room-scale experiences designed without space constraints exclude users in smaller rooms and create safety hazards.
